title: Macramé wind chime hanger
subtitle: A knotted frame to hold ceramic, shell, or driftwood wind chime elements
excerpt: Work a short knotted panel that holds five to seven pendants for a wind chime. The panel hangs from a single dowel and can take ceramic discs, shells, glass beads, or driftwood pieces as the chime elements. The finished hanger is about 30 cm wide and 25 cm long before pendants.
sourceNotes: Macramé wind chime holders synthesised from contemporary British macramé practice. The combination of a short knotted panel with pendant hanging cords is a standard beginner project.

glossaryTerms.definition: The main structural knot in macramé, made with two working cords tied around two filler cords in two steps. The basis of the knotted panel in this hanger.
The mounting knot for attaching cord to a dowel or ring. Fold the cord in half, loop the fold over the support, and pull both tails through the loop.
A wrapping knot used to bind a group of cords together at a single point. One cord wraps repeatedly around the bundle and the end is tucked through the wraps to secure.

content.text: This hanger consists of a short knotted panel with pendant cords hanging from the lower edge. The pendant cords carry the chime elements, which can be anything with a hole: shells, ceramic discs, glass droplets, or small pieces of driftwood. Use 3 mm cord for a finer, lighter look, or 5 mm for a chunkier one.

items.name: 3 mm or 4 mm single-strand cotton macramé cord
Chime elements with pre-drilled holes (shells, ceramic pieces, etc.)

content.text: Cut 20 cords each 1.8 m long. Fold each in half and mount on the dowel using
, spacing them evenly across a 30 cm span. This gives 40 working ends.
with 2 cm spacing between rows. This produces a short knotted panel about 6 to 8 cm deep from the dowel.
After the last square-knot row, group the cords into 5 to 7 bundles of equal numbers. Work a

around each bundle to bind it tightly about 3 cm below the last square-knot row. Leave the cord tails hanging below the gathering knot as the pendant drop cord. Thread each chime element onto the bundle tail before tying a final overhand knot to hold it in place. Trim the cord ends below the overhand knot to 5 cm.
Cut a 60 cm length of cord and tie it to both ends of the dowel to make a hanging loop. Suspend the wind chime in a spot where air movement will set the elements in motion.

items.problem: The pendant cords twist together and prevent the chimes from hanging freely
items.cause: The cords were twisted in the gathering knot, or the mounting spacing was too close
items.fix: Spread the pendant bundles further apart at the dowel mounting stage. A 5 cm spacing between bundle centres allows freedom of movement.
